summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a fiery Fall ’79 run with standout Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World and high‑energy Shakedown Street/High Time sequences.
review: The recording is an excellent, crisp soundboard source that lets every instrument shine – Phil’s bass is front‑and‑center and Brent’s keyboards cut through nicely. The performance epitomizes the Fall ’79 groove: a smoking first set followed by an even hotter second set highlighted by a ripping Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World, a blistering Shakedown Street → Samson/High Time stretch, and memorable moments from Mexicali Blues to Black Peter.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive comments on both the musical performance and the recording quality; only a few outlier low‑star reviews, so overall rating reflects an exceptional show with excellent sound.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp mix with great depth; Phil’s bass is upfront, Brent’s keyboards distinct, no reported drop‑outs or muddiness.

standout_songs: Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as "smoking" and second set kicking it up a notch, with a pre‑drum run featuring an especially fast Eyes of the World; overall flow energetic and cohesive.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry ripping his guitar, letting loose with fast driven noodling and a fire in his belly.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for being upfront and center, driving the train with great sound and depth.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s playing is highlighted as having his own distinct sound and contributing to the strong overall mix.
